confirmed.title: react README claims Inertia 2 peerDependency, but package.json requires Inertia 3
confirmed.evidence: packages-js/react/package.json line 68-69 declares peerDependency "@inertiajs/react": "^3.0.0"; CLAUDE.md/ADR-001 also state Inertia 3 is the only PHP<->React bridge.
confirmed.fix: Change 'peerDependencies para React 19 + Inertia 2' to 'React 19 + Inertia 3'.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. packages-js/react/README.md:46 states "peerDependencies para React 19 + Inertia 2 (não bundla)", but packages-js/react/package.json lines 63-66 declare peerDependencies "@inertiajs/react": "^3.0.0" (Inertia 3), also matching devDependencies (line 69) and CLAUDE.md/ADR-001 which mandate Inertia 3 as the only PHP<->React bridge. The doc says Inertia 2, the code requires Inertia 3 — a genuine contradiction. This file+line is not covered by any swept family (swept families cover PHP reference docs, Artisan cmd lists, trilingual guides, cloud env vars, types subpath entry points, widgets SKILL.md, hooks README). Fix: change "Inertia 2" to "Inertia 3" on line 46.

confirmed.title: ui README install command lists @base-ui-components/react, which is no longer a runtime/peer dependency
confirmed.evidence: packages-js/ui/package.json lists radix-ui (^1.4.3) as the real UI dependency and does NOT declare @base-ui-components/react as a peerDependency (it is only a leftover devDependency); no src file imports @base-ui-components/react, and ui/SKILL.md lines 16 & 97 state Base UI was replaced by radix-ui and must not be imported. The peerDependencies also include recharts, which the install line omits.
confirmed.fix: Remove @base-ui-components/react from the `pnpm add` line (Radix UI ships inside @arqel-dev/ui via the radix-ui dependency); add recharts to match the declared peerDependencies.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. packages-js/ui/README.md:17's `pnpm add` line tells users to install `@base-ui-components/react`, but that package is NOT a runtime dependency or peerDependency in package.json — it appears only as a leftover devDependency (line 120), the real UI primitive dep is `radix-ui ^1.4.3` (line 103, a true dependency), and no src file imports @base-ui-components/react (grep returned nothing). SKILL.md lines 16 & 97 explicitly state radix-ui replaced Base UI and it must not be imported, directly contradicting the install line. Additionally, package.json peerDependencies declares `recharts ^2.15.0` (line 114), which 5 src chart files actually import (LineChart/AreaChart/PieChart/RadarChart/BarChart), yet README.md:17 omits recharts from the install line — so a consumer following the README would install a dead/deprecated package while missing a required peer. This file+line is not covered by any SWEPT ledger family (those are PHP setter sigs, Artisan cmd lists, trilingual guide drift, cloud env vars/config, types subpath entries, widgets StatWidget setters, hooks README table). Actionable fix: drop @base-ui-components/react and add recharts to the install line.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED, NEW contradiction. packages/ai/SKILL.md line 73 sits under the "### Por chegar" (upcoming/unbuilt) section and lists "**Fields React follow-up**: `AiTranslateInput.tsx`, `AiSelectInput.tsx`, `AiExtractInput.tsx`" as work still to come. So the doc labels shipped, registered, tested components as unbuilt. Not covered by any swept family (not a php reference setter/method list, README Artisan commands, trilingual guide drift, cloud env-vars/config keys, types subpath entries, R10 StatWidget setter list, or R10 hooks README table). Actionable fix: remove that bullet or move the three components to a shipped/delivered section.

verdict.reason: CONFIRMED and NEW. packages/ai/SKILL.md:200 says "o React (follow-up) dispara POST .../translate", where "(follow-up)" implies the React front-end for AiTranslateField is not yet built. Code contradicts this: packages-js/ai/src/AiTranslateInput.tsx exists (11.5KB), is exported from packages-js/ai/src/index.ts (lines 33-37), is registered into the FieldRegistry via registerField('AiTranslateInput', LazyAiTranslateInput) in packages-js/ai/src/register.tsx:216-219, and has a test file AiTranslateInput.test.tsx. So the front-end is shipped, registered, and tested — the "(follow-up)" parenthetical is stale/inaccurate. This "follow-up" staleness family is NOT in any SWEPT family (SWEPT covers php reference setter signatures, Artisan command lists, trilingual reference-doc drift, cloud env vars/config keys, packages-js/types subpath entry points, StatWidget setters, and the hooks table — none touch packages/ai/SKILL.md's follow-up labels). No prior docs-audit round touched packages/ai/SKILL.md's follow-up markers. Actionable. NOTE: the same false "(follow-up)" label recurs at lines 38-40 (endpoint table), 73 ("Fields React follow-up: AiTranslateInput.tsx, AiSelectInput.tsx, AiExtractInput.tsx" — all three exist and are registered), and 231 (AiSelect classify prose); the proposed line-200-only fix is correct but under-scoped — the full fix should remove the stale follow-up markers across all these lines since AiSelectInput.tsx and AiExtractInput.tsx are likewise shipped, exported, and registered.
